behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all function names (resolveGatewayRequestedOperatorScopes, authorizeOperatorScopesForMethod, applyOwnerOnlyToolPolicy), file paths, deny list tools, 'agent' method, 'forbidden' type, 'missing scope' message, and 10+ entry requirement.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction effectively dictates the implementation: exact function names to import, exact argument values (`authorizeOperatorScopesForMethod("agent", requestedScopes)`, `applyOwnerOnlyToolPolicy(tools, false)`), exact error type string 'forbidden', exact message substring 'missing scope', exact list of 7 tools to add — leaving almost no reasoning about WHAT to change.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_owner_only_filtering_order requires a specific variable pattern `const <var> = applyOwnerOnlyToolPolicy(...)` followed by `<var>.filter(...!gatewayDenySet.has)`, rejecting valid alternatives like inline chaining or different variable-assignment styles.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests transpile and execute dangerous-tools.ts via Node to inspect the actual exported array; handler tests are text-based but deny-list tests execute code.

test_not_tautological.reason: Deny-list tests execute the module and check membership; handler tests require specific imports and call sites that a stub could not satisfy while also passing the behavioral deny-list tests.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All three bug areas (scope auth, owner-only filtering, deny list) are covered by corresponding tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with header comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass deny list, handler authorization wiring, Pass-to-pass).

structured_data_schema.reason: No structured data output; task is code-behavior.

no_typos.reason: Filenames and identifiers in instruction match those used in tests and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names exact symbols and literal argument values ('agent', false) and lists the 7 exact tool names to add — this is prescriptive implementation guidance beyond describing what's broken.
